repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 — generic non-Gaussian cumulant direction recovery, not SCORE score-Jacobian geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score — establishes the diagonal score-Jacobian leaf characterization; the mirror two-point instantiation is the added specialization
rolland2022score supplies the SCORE functional and chernoff1952measure supplies the local-testing machinery; neither states this map, but the fixed-path expansion is a narrow specialization
chernoff1952measure gives the simple-versus-simple product-measure exponent; conditional on Theorem 1, this application to one constructed pair is direct
hoyer2008nonlinear — the linear-Gaussian additive model is the reversible exceptional boundary
koehler2023efficiency studies score-matching versus likelihood for density-estimation parameters, not the specified causal-order testing detector and exponent

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1, Theorem 2, and Open-ended Question 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The related work omits the closer Montagna et al. (2023) CLeaR paper montagna23b, Scalable Causal Discovery with Score Matching (DAS), which directly uses the second derivative of the log-likelihood in nonlinear additive-Gaussian models and must be distinguished from the local SCORE-gap/Chernoff frontier.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The likelihood exponent concerns only the explicitly constructed two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM subexperiment, not a global nonlinear-ANM minimax class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact likelihood order-testing frontier conditional on the mirror-symmetric subexperiment and its stated regularity assumptions.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / ass:jacobian-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Any feasible-score conclusion is conditional on cross-fitted L4 estimability and r_n=o(C_m(delta_n)); no claim is made for all ANM laws or neural-score implementations.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The question asks whether the specified detector attains the likelihood exponent under the declared relative nuisance-rate premise.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add and accurately position Montagna et al. (2023), Scalable Causal Discovery with Score Matching (DAS): distinguish its second-score-derivative graph-recovery/pruning result from the mirror experiment’s local Chernoff calibration.

Turn the feasible rung into a decisive result by either giving a finite-dimensional score-sieve regime that verifies the stated nuisance rate and detector exponent, or proving an exponent loss for the declared detector.
Extend the single mirror path to a normalized nondegenerate Hermite-tangent class with uniform score-gap and information constants, or prove that C_m alone cannot support such a class-wide frontier.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision: incorporate and non-overlap DAS before advancing; the conditional mirror-experiment kernel remains field-tier if that survey repair is made.
